entries.title: Engineering Is Critical to Boosting Food Security
entries.summary: Nearly 750 million people face hunger today, according to the U.N. World Food Program . And by 2050, global demand for food is expected to increase by 50 percent from 2010 levels , the World Resources Institute says. entries.summary: Chile’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a fourth straight meeting, saying inflation risks have become more balanced as the economy weakens and a fuel-price shock remains contained.

entries.title: Warsh Faces First Big Test as Fed Chair
entries.summary: Federal Reserve policymakers are expected to hold interest rates steady on Wednesday, posing an early test for new chairman Kevin Warsh as rising inflation erodes households’ purchasing power and President Donald Trump continues to press for lower borrowing costs.

entries.title: Why Restoring Normal Traffic Through Hormuz Won’t Be Easy
entries.summary: The US and Iran have committed to reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most important artery for shipping oil and natural gas, which has been largely blocked since the two countries went to war in February. entries.title: How the Iran War Is Leaving Lasting Scars Across Asia
entries.summary: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, we discuss how war in Iran hit Asia especially hard and why structural economic problems are likely to persist, even with a peace deal in the works.

entries.title: US Exports to Cuba Jump as Fuel, Cars and Food Get Past Trump’s Blockade
entries.summary: US exports to Cuba have surged dramatically this year as businesses use a trade loophole to get fuel, appliances, furniture, food and vehicles past President Donald Trump’s wall of sanctions.

entries.title: Citi Chief US Economist Defends Unpopular Fed Rate-Cut Call
entries.summary: Citigroup Inc.’s Andrew Hollenhorst said the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates this year as the labor market weakens and oil prices ease, even as bond traders and his colleagues across Wall Street wager on hikes.
